title: Business plans
summary: 9609 AS — business plan contents, purpose for lenders/investors, and limitations.
learningObjectives: Outline main sections of a business plan.
Explain purposes for entrepreneurs and lenders.
Analyse role of market research in planning.

simpleExplanation.title: Road map for a new venture
simpleExplanation.summary: A business plan sets mission, objectives, market analysis, operations, and financial forecasts. It guides the owner and persuades banks or investors — but forecasts can be wrong.

flashcards.back: Guide owner; secure finance from bank/investors; clarify strategy.
Summary, mission, market, marketing, ops, management, finances.
Shows when cash shortfalls occur — survival issue (5.3.1).
Forecasts based on assumptions — market may change.
Emphasise repayment ability, collateral, realistic cash flow.
Emphasise growth potential, return on investment, exit strategy.
Plan quantifies start-up and working capital needs.

sections.content: **Business plans** (1.1.3) — exam questions ask **contents**, **purpose**, or **evaluate usefulness**. Link sections to **finance** and **marketing** topics.

sections.question: Start-up gym seeks $80k bank loan. Which three business plan sections matter most to the bank and why?
sections.solution:
**1. Cash-flow forecast (5.3.1):** Bank needs proof of **monthly cash** to cover **loan repayments** — membership seasonality must show no persistent negative months.

**2. Market research (3.2):** Evidence **local demand**, **competition**, realistic **membership targets** — reduces default risk.

**3. Management/experience:** Owner's **fitness industry track record** — human capital reduces failure risk.

**Limitation to acknowledge:** **Optimistic membership** assumptions — bank may stress-test 20% below forecast.
